name: QRadar-0004 - Lateral Movement via SMB Admin Shares
description: Detects lateral movement activity where a single host initiates SMB (port 445) connections to multiple internal hosts within a short period. This pattern is commonly associated with worm propagation, ransomware spread, and adversary lateral movement using tools like PsExec, WMI, or Pass-the-Hash techniques.

detection.log_source_type: Network Flows (QFlow), Windows Event Logs, Firewall
detection.event_name_filter: SMB Connection, Port 445, Admin Share Access
detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, COUNT(DISTINCT destinationIP) as unique_targets, COUNT(*) as smb_connections FROM flows WHERE destinationPort = 445 AND protocolid = 6 AND IsInternal(destinationIP) = TRUE AND IsInternal(sourceIP) = TRUE GROUP BY sourceIP HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT destinationIP) >= 10 LAST 10 MINUTES

response.offense_name: SMB Lateral Movement from {sourceIP} — {unique_targets} hosts targeted
response.offense_description: A single internal host has initiated SMB connections to 10 or more distinct internal hosts within 10 minutes. This is a strong indicator of worm propagation or adversary lateral movement.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rules: net_lateral_movement_smb and win_remote_service_smb. Whitelist backup systems, patch management servers, and file server replication. Correlate with Windows event 4624 (logon) and 4648 (explicit credential logon).
author: Security Team
